metadata.description: wBETH (Wrapped Binance Beacon ETH) is a liquid staking token issued by Binance, representing ETH staked through Binance's validator infrastructure. Users deposit ETH via the Binance platform and receive wBETH at an accruing exchange rate; on-chain, the contracts manage token issuance (WrapTokenV3ETH), the pending-withdrawal queue (UnwrapTokenV1ETH), and exchange rate updates (ExchangeRateUpdater). The backing ETH is held off-chain with Binance validators — only the withdrawal queue holds on-chain ETH ($25.85K), while the token itself has a market capitalization of $8.12B. All contracts are upgradeable ZeppelinOS proxies controlled exclusively by Binance EOAs; there is no timelock, multisig, or on-chain governance.

admins.name: Proxy Admin (EOA)
admins.description: EOA holding the ZeppelinOS proxy admin role for both WrapTokenV3ETH and UnwrapTokenV1ETH. Can replace the proxy admin and upgrade both contract implementations, with the ability to alter all protocol logic or redirect user funds. Controls $2.18M in direct capital exposure.

admins.name: Protocol Owner (EOA)
admins.description: Omnibus Binance EOA holding the owner, pauser, masterMinter, and blacklister roles across WrapTokenV3ETH, UnwrapTokenV1ETH, and ExchangeRateUpdater. Can pause all token transfers, configure minting allowances, blacklist any address, replace the exchange rate oracle and operator, and transfer ownership of all three contracts. Controls $2.18M in direct capital exposure.

admins.name: OperatorWallet (Minter)
admins.description: Upgradeable proxy contract holding the sole minting authorization for WrapTokenV3ETH. Executes all on-chain wBETH issuance and burning; its behavior is determined by the OperatorWallet implementation, which is separately upgradeable by a distinct proxy admin EOA.

admins.name: Zero Address (Revoked)
admins.description: The rescuer role on WrapTokenV3ETH is set to the zero address, disabling the rescueERC20 function. No tokens can be rescued from the token contract in the current state.

admins.name: ExchangeRateUpdater
admins.description: Immutable contract that mediates all exchange rate updates to WrapTokenV3ETH. Configured callers may update the wBETH:ETH rate subject to a per-caller allowance cap and a replenishment interval, limiting the magnitude of any single update.

mitigations.label: rate-limited-update
mitigations.description: Rate updates are bounded per caller by ExchangeRateUpdater's allowance system. Each configured caller has a maxAllowances ceiling replenished at a fixed interval; a single updateExchangeRate call can only move the rate by the caller's current remaining allowance. Sustained large-scale manipulation requires holding caller access across many replenishment intervals.

admins.name: Operator (EOA)
admins.description: Binance operational EOA serving as operator on WrapTokenV3ETH, operatorAddress on UnwrapTokenV1ETH, and the sole configured caller on ExchangeRateUpdater. Controls ETH flows between contracts (moveToStakingAddress, moveToUnwrapAddress), manages the withdrawal queue (setLockTime, moveToBackAddress), and submits exchange rate updates. Controls $2.18M in direct capital exposure.

mitigations.label: rate-limited-update
mitigations.description: Rate updates are bounded per caller by ExchangeRateUpdater's allowance system. Each configured caller has a maxAllowances ceiling replenished at a fixed interval; a single updateExchangeRate call can only move the rate by the caller's current remaining allowance. Sustained large-scale manipulation requires holding caller access across many replenishment intervals.

Only a floor constraint (MIN_LOCK_TIME = 172 800 s, 2 days) is enforced. No upper bound exists in the contract — setting lockTime to uint256_max permanently prevents claimWithdraw from succeeding for every pending request, locking all queued ETH indefinitely.

admins.name: WrapTokenV3ETH
admins.description: Internal call source for UnwrapTokenV1ETH's requestWithdraw and moveFromWrapContract functions. These are protocol-internal bridges invoked only by WrapTokenV3ETH's own logic; no external party can trigger them independently of the token contract.

admins.name: Recharge Address (EOA)
admins.description: EOA configured as the recharge address on UnwrapTokenV1ETH. When the operator calls rechargeFromRechargeAddress, ETH is pulled from this address into the withdrawal queue to cover pending claims.

admins.name: OperatorWallet Proxy Admin (EOA)
admins.description: EOA holding the ZeppelinOS proxy admin role for OperatorWallet. Can upgrade the OperatorWallet implementation, replacing all minting and operational logic of the authorized minter.

admins.name: OperatorWallet Owner (EOA)
admins.description: Owner of the OperatorWallet proxy. Can add and remove hot wallets, change the operator address, pause or unpause the wallet, and transfer ownership of OperatorWallet.

admins.name: OperatorWallet Operator (EOA)
admins.description: Active operator within OperatorWallet. Can call mint on WrapTokenV3ETH via OperatorWallet.mint to issue new wBETH, and transfer tokens from the OperatorWallet's own holdings.

funds.name: WrapTokenV3ETH (wBETH)
funds.description: The wBETH token contract. Tracks a market capitalization of $8.12B; the backing ETH is not held on-chain but with Binance validators off-chain.

funds.name: UnwrapTokenV1ETH (ETH)
funds.description: Withdrawal queue contract holding $25.85K in ETH pending redemption. Users' withdrawal requests are queued here; the operator allocates ETH and claimants call claimWithdraw once the lock period elapses.

mitigations.label: rate-limited-update
mitigations.description: Rate updates are bounded per caller by ExchangeRateUpdater's allowance system. Each configured caller has a maxAllowances ceiling replenished at a fixed interval; a single updateExchangeRate call can only move the rate by the caller's current remaining allowance. Sustained large-scale manipulation requires holding caller access across many replenishment intervals.

Only a floor constraint (MIN_LOCK_TIME = 172 800 s, 2 days) is enforced. No upper bound exists in the contract — setting lockTime to uint256_max permanently prevents claimWithdraw from succeeding for every pending request, locking all queued ETH indefinitely.

governance.proposalRequirements: No on-chain governance. All privileged roles are Binance-controlled EOAs with no timelock or multisig.
governance.votingProcess: All protocol changes executed directly by Binance-controlled EOAs. No voting, no timelock, no DAO.

codeAndAudits.title: Code & Audits
codeAndAudits.description: Smart contract analysis and audit history
